entry.title: Chart of Accounts in Parallel Line - Test environement (SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 2602)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope you are doing well.

We are currently working on a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition greenfield implementation and we are facing an unexpected behavior related to the Chart of Accounts.

In the main line, we modified the Chart of Accounts in the Customizing tenant (creation of new G/L accounts using the copy functionality and updates of descriptions). All these changes correctly generated transport requests.

We then created a branch to copy these changes to the Customizing tenant of the parallel line. After the branch, we noticed that:

- All Chart of Accounts changes are visible in the parallel line Customizing tenant
- However, no transport requests exist in that tenant

We are now facing an issue because we want to perform SIT in the parallel line test tenant, but the Chart of Accounts (and some related configurations) are not available in that system.

Has anyone faced a similar issue, or are there any guidelines on how this situation should be handled in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Any help would be highly appreciated.

Kind regards,
Alexandre B
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Re: BATCH DETERMINATION IS FAILED
entry.content: Hi @MrAdilsonBenevides , in addition to @Amit_SAP's excellent points, I suggest checking your Selection Criteria within the Strategy Records (COB1/COB2). Sometimes, if the characteristics defined in the selection class don't perfectly match the batch attributes in the warehouse, the system will fail to find a valid batch even if stock exists. It's also worth checking if the 'Check Instructions' in Availability Check are bypassing the batch logic.

While the field exists, if you are revising your interfaces now, it might be a good time to look into using OData services or API Business Hub equivalents for these subsystems. Relying on direct table fields like APLZL is still common, but moving toward standard APIs will make your future upgrades much smoother and align better with the 'Clean Core' strategy.

entry.title: External Billing API – credit memo equivalent of EOX1/EDX1/CIX1?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We use BillingDocumentRequest_In (SAP_COM_0120, scope 1Z6) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to create billing documents from an external source system, without any preceding sales order in S/4HANA.

The invoice case works with EOX1 / EDX1 / CIX1.

For credit memos we need the equivalent combination but BillingDocumentType G2 with EOX1 / EDX1 fails with VF 002 (“Cannot bill SD documents of type EOX1 using billing type G2”), and we cannot find a delivered EOXC / EDXC or any other external credit-memo document type in customizing.

Please let us know:
  • Which SalesDocumentType, SalesDocumentItemCategory and BillingDocumentType should be sent in BillingDocumentRequest_In to create a credit memo without a preceding sales order (i.e. the counterpart of EOX1 / EDX1 / CIX1)?
  • If no such combination is delivered, what is the supported alternative for posting external credit memos in Public Cloud?

entry.title: Re: Catching Bounce Mails
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is no native bounce‑mail handling for customer invoice emails. The system’s “Monitoring Email Transmissions” app only confirms that the message was handed over to the mail server, not that it reached the recipient’s inbox. To detect invalid addresses or bounced mails, you need to rely on external mail server feedback or custom monitoring

Options to handle bounce mails

1) Use External Mail Server Logs
If your tenant is connected to Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, or another SMTP relay, check their bounce reports. These systems provide detailed Non‑Delivery Reports (NDRs) that can be monitored centrally.

2) Custom Integration
Build a small middleware (e.g., SAP BTP app or CPI iFlow) to capture bounce notifications from the mail server. Feed this back into SAP (e.g., update customer master data or trigger workflow alerts).

entry.title: Re: Apply Customer Down Payment in Debit Memo Request
entry.content: Hi @Johannes_Wienho , to build on Lakshmipathi's suggestion, if you are following a Clean Core strategy in S/4HANA Cloud, the custom output enhancement (via Adobe Forms/Output Management) is indeed your best bet for the visual representation. For the accounting side, ensure that the 'Down Payment' indicator in the Billing Document type is configured, though for DMRs, the link is often strictly a financial clearing task in FI-AR (Transaction F-32 or automatic clearing) rather than an SD-driven one. Have you checked if using Item Category TATX (for Down Payments) provides the necessary pricing integration you need?

entry.title: Re: Catching Bounce Mails
entry.content: Hi @FSCHMITT-MANTH3Y, to expand on Lakshmipathi's points, if you're looking for a 'Clean Core' compliant way to handle this, the SAP BTP route is highly recommended. You can set up an iFlow that listens for bounce notifications from your mail provider and uses the Business Partner API to automatically flag the email address as 'Invalid' or trigger a 'Data Maintenance' task for your master data team. This prevents the issue from recurring in the next billing cycle.

entry.title: Assistance Required – Date Format Handling in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

We need your assistance for the following query:

We are loading data from S/4HANA to Datasphere, where date-related fields are initially received in string format (e.g., 20251231 in YYYYMMDD format). We are converting these using the TO_DATE() function, where Date field ID results in the format 2025-12-31 (YYYY-MM-DD) and its behaving as per Datasphere default format.

Here, business expectation on Date ID format is 31.12.2025 (DD.MM.YYYY) and as per our knowledge we can change only the description by user-profile date format settings but not the Key-Value (2025-12-31 (YYYY-MM-DD)).

So, here business is expecting is there any option to change the Date key value format too into 31.12.2025 (DD.MM.YYYY) ?

Could you please review this and advise on possible solutions?

Regards,
Radhakrushna

entry.title: Re: Why do SAP Customer Data Cloud (CDC) child sites not...
entry.content:
Hi @gerstmaier15 and team. The reason Hosted Pages remain centralized is to preserve the 'Single Identity' integrity across a site group, ensuring a seamless SSO experience.

However, to solve your requirement for distinct branding per child site, you don't necessarily need the feature 'on' the child site. You should leverage the Screen-Set logic where you can pass a siteID or apiKey context to the Hosted Page. By using conditional CSS and localized Screen-Sets managed at the Parent level, you can dynamically trigger different branding and user experiences based on which child site initiated the login.

If you are on the latest 2024/2025 release, check the Console for the 'Multi-Branding' enhancements in the Hosted Pages editor, which allows you to map specific sub-domains to unique UI configurations without breaking the parent-child inheritance.

entry.title: Re: Where to find the SAML URLs for Entra / SAP SuccessFa...
entry.content:
Hi @LOJ Loïc. The most reliable way to find these URLs is through the Tenant Settings of your IAS console rather than searching for generic addresses online.

  1. From IAS to Entra ID: Go to Applications & Resources > Tenant Settings > SAML 2.0 Configuration. Here, you can download the Metadata XML. This file contains your Entity ID, Single Sign-On URL, and the Signing Certificate required for the Entra ID side.

  2. From SuccessFactors to IAS: In the SuccessFactors Provisioning or Admin Center (SAML Outside Services), you will find the SuccessFactors Metadata. Uploading this into your IAS 'Applications' section will automatically populate the Reply URL (ACS) and Audience/Identifier.

If manual entry is absolutely required, the Identifier is typically your IAS tenant URL (e.g., https://<tenant_id>.accounts.ondemand.com) and the Reply URL follows the pattern https://<tenant_id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/acs/<tenant_id>. However, always use the 'Upload Metadata' option in both Entra and IAS to ensure the endpoints match your specific region and tenant ID perfectly.

entry.title: 🎉 📣We are LIVE with the new SAP Security Administrator (C_SEC) Practical Exam!!!!!📣 🎉
entry.content:
Do you remember the days when you would revise every major bullet point from a training course booklet and try to guess what the 80 multiple choice questions may look like (or even going looking for leaks online? Naughty!)? As a job head-hunter, you may have looked at the candidates’ qualifications and wondered if they actually have the proven practical experience enlisted on their resume, or if it is just certified theoretical knowledge?

Well now, in a fast-paced world where knowledgeable experts with practical experience need to stand out from the crowd, SAP have now revolutionized how you prove your expertise!

For those in the SAP Authorization and Security field, the SAP Certified – Security Administrator (C_SEC) exam has officially transitioned to a new system-based practical exam format.

This means you won’t just answer theoretical questions about which transaction code or app is used to maintain Fiori Catalogs—instead, you’ll be tasked with performing real-world security administrative scenarios like creating SAP Fiori Business Roles and managing Authorizations for end users on a live SAP environment. It’s an open-book, real-world challenge designed to prove you are job-ready from day one.

🛠️ Validate Your Skills
Whether you are looking to earn your first badge or prove your mastery as a SAP Security administrator, these are your essential links:
▪️ Prepare with the Learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/managing-user-access-and-security-of-sap-s-4-hana-and-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition
▪️ Take the Practical Exam: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-security-administrator 

💡 What is "SAP Certification Reimagined"?
SAP has transformed its entire certification portfolio to reflect the modern AI era. The Certification Reimagined initiative introduces:
▪️ System-Based Exams: Hands-on tasks in a live, time-boxed SAP environment.
▪️ Scenario-Based Assessments: AI-driven roleplays to test your consulting and architectural advice.
▪️ Open-Book Format: You can use SAP Joule, the SAP Help Portal and SAP Learning during the exam - just like you would on the job.

entry.title: 💡 How to prepare for the SAP C_SEC certification exam 💡
entry.content:
Want to become a SAP Certified Security Administrator and not sure how to prepare for the exam? Come and join us in our "Get Certified" Live Sessions, where expert trainers will support you by helping you understand the new certification exam format and interface. These sessions will explain the learning journey self-study content and guide you with tips for preparation.

There will be a kick-off session outlining the series ahead, which will then be followed by corresponding content review sessions covering important certification-relevant topics from the courses that are part of the corresponding learning journey. 

These sessions complement your self-study, providing you with the opportunity to get explanations and demonstrations from an expert, who can answer questions you have about the content, helping you to be better prepared for the certification.

The kick-off sessions are planned to run multiple times in the year, with the individual topic content review sessions running on a regular basis, so you can sign up to these sessions according to your own schedule.

For more information, check out the link below, where you can also sign up for the sessions.

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-sap-certified-security-administrator

entry.title: Mastering the Market: Seamless FX Risk Management for Commodity Businesses
entry.content:
Commodity trading operates at the intersection of global markets, currencies, and constant price movements. While price fluctuations in physical goods are a known challenge, the hidden sting of Foreign Exchange (FX) risk can often be the difference between a profitable deal and a significant loss.

We are excited to introduce a new, integrated function within SAP Commodity Management designed to automate and simplify how commodity businesses handle currency exposure.

The Challenge: Visibility in a Multi-Currency World
Commodity desks often struggle to identify the exact foreign currency impact arising from physical business transactions in real-time. Without a centralized way to track these risks, hedging remains reactive, leading to unexpected discrepancies and manual back-office overhead.

The Solution: FX Risk Management for Commodity Businesses
Our new solution bridges the gap between physical commodity management and financial treasury operations. By providing new hedge desk apps on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) integrated with SAP Commodity Management and SAP Agricultural Contract Management (ACM), businesses can now achieve end-to-end automation.

How It Works: The Process Flow
The system creates a "closed-loop" process that removes manual intervention at every critical step:
* Exposure Generation: FX risks are automatically identified and calculated from physical documents (like Purchase Orders) in SAP Commodity Management and SAP Agricultural Contract Management Trading Contracts, for all price and FX Rate fixed in Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine (CPE) related conditions.
* Automated Hedge Requests: Once an exposure is recorded, a hedge request is triggered in FX commodity hedge desk and trade request is released to SAP Treasury and Risk Management. (Connection to other TRM systems is also possible).
* SAP Trading Platform Integration (TPI): (optional) connected TPI system provides integrations with external trading platforms to receive quotes and execute trades.
* Optimized Hedge Desk Workflow: To minimize hedging costs, FX exposures with identical attributes can be aggregated into unified hedge requests. The end-to-end process from FX exposure generation to send out trade requests is fully automated and governed by customizable threshold controls to ensure precision and efficiency.
* On behalf of hedge scenario is supported.
* Full Circle Visibility: The finalized hedged rate is fed back into hedge requests, ensuring your hedge desk is always filled with accurate information.


Key Process Overview:

Why It Matters: The Bottom Line
This isn't just about cleaner data; it s about operational transformation:
* 90%* Efficiency Gain: Drastically reduce the time spent on back-office activities through automated workflows.
* Zero* Discrepancies: Eliminate up to 100% of unexpected discrepancies by moving from manual tracking to a centralized SAP system.
* Proactive Mitigation: Manage volatility centrally, ensuring that currency swings never catch your margins off-guard.

*based on assumptions from value engineering

Conclusion: Future-Proof Your Commodity Desk
In an era of global economic shifts, waiting for month-end reports to understand your currency exposure is a risk you can't afford. By automating the link between physical contracts and financial hedges, SAP empowers your team to focus on strategic trading rather than manual data entry.

Stop reacting to the market and start mastering it. With FX Risk Management for Commodity Businesses, you gain the precision needed to protect your margins and the agility to act on new opportunities with confidence.

@d059642

entry.title: IBP Exercise: Implicit Activation Prerequisite for Key Figure Calculations?
entry.content:
I am following an SAP IBP training exercise related to key figure calculations in a planning area (base level MTHPRODCUST). The exercise guides the creation of stored and calculated key figures and then instructs to activate the planning area.

Exercise instruction (simplified)

The documentation instructs to:

  • Use an existing stored key figure (e.g. CONSENSUSDEMANDQTY)
  • Create a calculated key figure (CONSENSUSDEMANDREV = Unit Price × Qty)
  • Activate the planning area

What I observe in the system

When following the steps literally:

  • CONSENSUSDEMANDQTY is defined as Stored
  • CONSENSUSDEMANDREV is defined as Calculated
  • Activation fails with an error indicating that the quantity key figure must be stored or calculated

After investigation, activation succeeds only if:

  • The stored base key figures are successfully activated first
  • And the calculated key figures are added afterwards, followed by another activation

Question / clarification

Is it correct that:

  • SAP IBP requires stored base key figures to be activated at least once before they can be referenced by calculated key figures?
  • And that this activation order is an implicit prerequisite not explicitly stated in the exercise documentation?

If so, would you recommend documenting this activation sequence explicitly in training material to avoid confusion in learner or clean tenants?

entry.title: Re: I am getting a warning message while opening a work o...
entry.content:
Hi @sankets1. To answer your second query: A material is defined as an Assembly in a work order when it is entered in the 'Assembly' field on the Header Data tab (near the Equipment/Functional Location fields) or at the Operation level.

How to set it for testing:

  1. Go to IW31 or IW32.

  2. On the Header Data tab, look for the 'Assembly' field.

  3. Enter your material number there. If that material has a deletion flag, you will immediately see the IM049 warning.

Why it's different from other orders: In your other orders, the materials are likely only listed on the Components tab. SAP typically does not trigger the IM049 'Assembly' warning for standard component consumption—it only triggers it when the material is used to define the technical structure of the work being performed (the Assembly field). Check the header of your affected order; you’ll likely find the material number entered in that specific 'Assembly' box, whereas in your other orders, that field is probably empty.

entry.title: Re: Impacts of SAP API Policy v4/2026 on existing custome...
entry.content:
Hi @JOGethmann and m1m. This policy update is the strongest signal yet that SAP is moving toward a 'Zero-Modification' mindset for the core.

To your questions on transition and prioritization:

  1. Positioning & Impact: SAP is positioning this as a technical debt issue. Non-compliant integrations are now 'at-risk' because SAP reserves the right to change or decommission internal APIs without notice in any release cycle.

  2. Transition Strategy: There is rarely a global 'grace period' for APIs. Instead, the strategy should be 'Event-Driven Remediation.' Whenever an existing integration requires maintenance or a connected system is upgraded, it should be migrated to a published API from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

  3. Prioritization Logic: Customers should prioritize based on Business Criticality and Upgrade Frequency. If you are on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, remediation is urgent because you have less control over the upgrade timeline.

Best Practice: For gaps where no published API exists, do not stick with the internal one. Instead, use the SAP Customer Influence portal to request the API or build a 'Clean Core' wrapper using SAP BTP and ADT (ABAP Development Tools) to expose the required data via a stable OData/REST service. This moves the 'non-compliance' from the SAP core to a managed BTP layer, which is much safer for upgrades.

entry.title: Re: Configuration for document type for Bank statements (...
entry.content:
Hi @SuyashG. Dawid is correct—in the Public Cloud environment, the flexibility to change document types for bank statements is strictly limited to ensure 'Clean Core' stability.

Why it is set to 'SA': SAP delivers these posting rules as standard content. Because the logic is shared across the tenant, allowing manual changes to document types could disrupt automated clearing and reconciliation processes.

What you can verify: You should still navigate to CBC / SSCUI 101024 (Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement) to ensure your external MT940 transaction codes are mapped to the correct Internal Posting Rules.

If 'SA' is a dealbreaker: If your business requirements absolutely demand a different document type (e.g., for specific reporting or filtering), this is considered a non-standard expert configuration. You would need to submit a request to SAP for review, or consider using SAP BTP to build a 'side-by-side' extension that reclassifies the documents after posting, though sticking to the standard 'SA' is highly recommended to avoid future upgrade issues.

entry.title: Re: IAS: loginName whitespace handling (lack of normaliza...
entry.content:
Hi @BFLXR. This is an excellent catch and a very sophisticated observation regarding identity consistency.

From an architectural standpoint, the preservation of whitespace is often a byproduct of ensuring the loginName can support legacy systems that may have used unusual string patterns, but as you pointed out, it creates significant risk for modern SSO/IDP setups.

Why this happens: SAP IAS treats the loginName as a unique string literal. Because it doesn't enforce a specific schema (unlike an email address), the backend treats a space as a valid character.

The Path Forward: MatthiasL is correct—this behavior is best addressed through an Influence Request. SAP's Identity and Access Management (IAM) team prioritizes these 'normalization' rules based on community demand, especially when they impact Identity Provisioning (IPS) and downstream S/4HANA or SuccessFactors correlations.

In the meantime: If you are using Identity Provisioning (IPS) to feed IAS, I highly recommend implementing a Transformation Rule using the trim() function. This ensures that any identity synced from an external source (like Active Directory) is sanitized before it hits the IAS data layer, preventing the 'invisible' identity mismatches you've described.

entry.title: Re: Unable to get file picker in pop-up of RAP application
entry.content:
Hi @sh4il3sh. You are correct that the issue lies in the metadata representation. For the Fiori elements UI to render a file picker in a RAP action popup, the field must be exposed as a Stream, not just binary data.

How to fix this:

  1. Annotation check: Ensure your Abstract Entity includes the @Semantics.largeObject and @Semantics.mimeType annotations correctly. However, for RAP actions, you specifically need the Stream binding.

  2. Refine the Abstract Entity: In RAP, to ensure the field is treated as a stream in the Action's parameter, you must explicitly link the MimeType field.

Try this structure:

define abstract entity Z_S_ExcelUpload {
  @Semantics.largeObject: { 
      mimeType: 'MimeType', 
      contentDispositionPreference: #INLINE
  }
  attachment : abap.rawstring(0); // Use (0) for dynamic length
  
  @Semantics.mimeType: true
  mimetype   : abap.char(128);
  
  filename   : abap.char(128); // Optional but recommended
}

Why it's still not showing: Even with the correct annotations, OData V2 often struggles to render LOBs in popups via Abstract Entities. Since you have moved to OData V4, you are on the right path. Ensure your Service Definition and Binding are refreshed.

Important Tip: In the UI, the file picker only appears if the action is defined as a factory action or a specific instance-bound action that correctly maps these parameters. If the 'attachment' field in your popup remains a simple text/hex input, it means the UI hasn't recognized the @Semantics.largeObject link to the mimetype field. Double-check that your Action definition in the Behavior Definition (BDEF) mentions the abstract entity as a parameter.

entry.title: Re: The electronic signature is not working in the SAP DM...
entry.content:
Hi @SSP1995. Since you’ve confirmed the functionality works in POD 1.1, this strongly points to a change in how Order POD 2.0 handles service registry or data binding for the Electronic Signature Plugin.

Key areas to check before raising a ticket:

  1. POD Designer Configuration: In Order POD 2.0, verify if the E-Signature plugin is correctly assigned to the specific Operation or Action button. Sometimes, version 2.0 requires the 'Routing' or 'SFC' context to be explicitly passed as a parameter in the plugin configuration, which was more automated in 1.1.

  2. Service Mapping: Since the Network tab shows missing routing details, check if the Production Process or the underlying OData service utilized by POD 2.0 has the necessary permissions to access the routing APIs.

  3. IAS/BTP Roles: Ensure that the user roles for POD 2.0 include any new scopes required for the upgraded services, as version 2.0 often utilizes different microservices for data retrieval.

Recommendation: Given that you've identified a discrepancy in the updated logic, you should definitely raise an SAP Support ticket under the component MFG-DM-ME-POD. Be sure to include the HAR file from your Network tab trace to prove that the routing details are failing to load in 2.0 despite working in 1.1.

entry.title: 1H 2026 Release - Snack view for Program displays different percentage complete than within the prog
entry.content:
Hello.  We noticed during 1H release testing that the snack view for a program displays a different percent complete than if we open the program.  Does anyone else think that SAP/SF should make them match vice having to submit an enhancement request?  This will be confusing to learners.

Based on their response to my incident/case - this is working by design.

Here is their response:

I discussed internally with Engineering team.
The behaviour observed in the newly introduced “Snack View” is expected and aligns with its current design logic.
The progress percentage displayed in the Snack View is based on section-level completion, consistent with how progress is calculated in the ILX bite card. This is why it reflects values such as “A out of B sections completed,” and may appear as 0% if no sections are fully completed yet.
In contrast, the Program Details page calculates progress based on overall program completion, which includes all individual items within the program. This results in a different percentage (e.g., 33%) even if no sections are fully completed.
Therefore, the discrepancy is due to two different progress calculation methods being used:
-----Card and Snack View → Section completion percentage
----- Program Details Page → Overall item-based completion percentage
This is not a defect but a difference in how progress is represented across components.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: New depreciation key customizing option not available
entry.content:
Hi @vibhor_bansal. It is important to note that the SPRO path mentioned by @kassettyvijaykiran is only applicable to On-Premise or Private Cloud systems and is not available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

How to handle Depreciation Keys in Public Cloud:

  1. Standard Keys: SAP provides a wide range of pre-configured depreciation keys based on the local Chart of Depreciation for your country. In most cases, you are expected to use these standard keys.

  2. Configuration Path: To view or manage these, you must use the SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) or the Manage Your Solution app. Look for SSCUI 102207 (Maintain Depreciation Keys).

  3. Customization Limits: If the standard keys do not meet your requirements, creating a brand-new depreciation key from scratch is often restricted. You typically 'Copy' an existing key and modify allowed parameters (such as the useful life) rather than building new calculation logic.

Recommendation: Check SSCUI 102207 first. If you truly need a calculation logic that isn't supported by the standard keys, you may need to submit an Expert Configuration request to SAP, as modifying core depreciation logic is a high-impact change in a multi-tenant environment.

entry.title: Re: GR ISSUE
entry.content:
Hi @Thirusap18. As Lakshmipathi noted, the most frequent cause for this error is that the Post Goods Issue (PGI) has not yet been completed for the outbound delivery.

How to resolve this:

  1. Check Delivery Status: Go to transaction VL03N (Display Outbound Delivery) for your delivery 80015177.

  2. Verify Goods Movement: Check the Document Flow. If you do not see a 'Goods Issue' document linked to the delivery, the supplying plant has not yet shipped the stock out of their inventory.

  3. Post the PGI: You must first perform the PGI (typically in VL02N) at the supplying plant.

  4. Perform GR: Once the PGI is posted and the status of the delivery changes to 'Completely Processed' for goods movement, you will be able to post the GR at the receiving plant without the error.

Other potential checks: If PGI is already posted, ensure that the Movement Type used in your STO (usually 101) is correctly mapped to the delivery item category in your shipping settings.

entry.title: 📣 Big News for SAP HANA Pros - The Practical Certification Eera is Here 📣
entry.content:
SAP is moving away from traditional multiple-choice questions toward performance-based, system-based practical exams. If you are looking to validate your skills as a Database Administrator, it’s no longer just about what you know, it’s about what you can do in a live environment.

The SAP Certified - Database Administrator - SAP HANA exam is now available in this new format. This means you'll be tasked with performing real-world administrative duties like deploying databases, configuration, backup, and setting up HA scenarios in an SAP HANA Cloud system.

🛠️ How to Upskill & Get Certified
Here are the essential resources to get you started:
▪️Prepare with the Learning Journey: Becoming a Certified Database Administrator - SAP HANA
▪️Take the Exam: SAP Certified - Database Administrator - SAP HANA

💡What is "SAP Certification Reimagined"?
SAP is transforming its entire certification portfolio to reflect the modern AI era. The Certification Reimagined initiative introduces:
▪️System-Based Exams: Hands-on tasks in a live, time-boxed SAP environment.
▪️Scenario-Based Assessments: AI-driven roleplays to test your consulting and architectural advice.
▪️Open-Book Format: You can use SAP Joule, the SAP Help Portal and SAP Learning during the exam - just like you would on the job.

🔗Learn more about the transformation here: Ready to get certified with SAP?

entry.title: Printing Direct Debit Mandate Confirmation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In the blog post you can learn about how to send direct debit mandate confirmation with your customized printing layout to payers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

What is direct debit mandate confirmation?

With this function, direct debit mandate confirmation can be generated automatically when you create mandates or change the key details. The payer can see and verify the current status of the mandates.

After reading this blog post, you can learn about how to set up your own printing layout or use the SAP pre-delivered one.

Prerequisites

Enabling the mandate confirmation function

Search for the Define General Settings configuration step (ID: 106087).
Select one option from the Print Mandate Letter Options as shown in the picture.
Note: If one of the options is chosen, the correspondence of type 0047 is automatically generated upon saving the mandates.

Select an option based on your requirement:

  • 01 – Print confirmation of new mandates
    You print and send the confirmation of a newly consented mandate to the payer.
  • 02 –  Print confirmation of changed mandates
    You print and send the confirmation of mandate changes to the payer.
  • 03 –  Print confirmation of new/changed mandate
    You print and send the confirmation of both newly consented mandates and the changed mandates to the payer.
  • 04 -  Print confirmation of mandates with mandate instructions
    You print and send the confirmation of the mandate changes to the payer and, at the same time, send the mandate instructions (mandate changes) to your bank.

(Optional) Activate Confirmation of Mandate Instructions

If you select print confirmation with mandate instruction, you have to select the instruction type in the Define Direct Debit Procedure (ID: 106086).

Note: To complete the settings below, you have to have the minimum authorization as the Configuration Expert - Business Process Configuration (SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT) business role template.

Assign Form Templates

Search for the Assign Form Templates configuration step (ID: 102313).
Create an entry as follows:

Application Object TypeObject TypeForm Template ID
FKC_DDM_MANDATE_CONFIRMATIONMANDATE_CONFIRMATIONFIN_CA_DDM_MNDT_CONFIRMATION

Assign Email Templates

Search for the Assign Email Templates configuration step (ID: 102313).
Create an entry as follows:

Application Object TypeObject TypeEmailTemplate ID
FKC_DDM_MANDATE_CONFIRMATIONMANDATE_CONFIRMATIONFIN_CA_DEFAULT_EMAIL_TEMPLATE

Master Data Settings

In the Maintain Business Partner app or Manage Business Partner Master Data app (F3163), make sure you have a business partner with an MKK – Contract Account role. Create an entry for email contact that is assigned for direct debit mandate as shown in the following picture.

Procedure of Printing Mandate Confirmations

Before printing direct debit mandate confirmations, you have either created a direct debit mandate or you create a direct debit instruction (change request of a direct debit mandate). For more information, see the following blog posts:

  • How to authorize Direct Debit Payments outside SEPA in Contract Accounting
  • Bank Communication Using Direct Debit Mandate in SAP S4/HANA Public Cloud

Make sure your direct debit mandate status is active.

  • Open the Display Correspondence app (F3693).
    Enter 0047 in the Correspondence Type field and select Go.
  • Select the correspondence.
  • Select the print button.
  • In the Print Correspondence options in the output mode:
    Test Print (0) – with this option you are not able to see the PDF in the display correspondence app.
    Real Print (X)
    Repeat Print (R)
  • In the Send Control field, choose how you will get the output:
    Only email
    External output management system
    Printout + email
    Only printout

With the printout options, you can see the PDF in the display correspondence app.
With the email options, you get an email that you assigned in the business partner master data with a PDF.

Select the Print button. Then you can view the PDF right away or receive it in your email, as long as you did not choose Test Print.

Is this blog post useful to you? Leave your comments below to let us know your thoughts.

entry.title: Cloud ERP SCM Product-centric Customer Council - Shaping the Future of AI-Powered Supply Chain
entry.content:
by Julia Zinovieva (Customer Officer Cloud ERP SCM)

Cloud ERP SCM Product-centric Customer Council - Shaping the Future of AI-Powered Supply Chain

On April 14–16, the Cloud ERP SCM team presented the latest developments and AI innovations at the Product-centric Customer Council in Berlin. Over 2.5 days, customers and SAP experts came together for an exciting and inspiring exchange of insights across key solution areas including SAP Integrated Product Development (IPD), Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC), Available-to-Promise (ATP), Asset Management, and PP/DS.

The agenda combined strategic perspectives, product deep dives, and interactive formats. Product Management did an excellent job explaining the roadmaps in their respective areas, demonstrating AI use cases, and enabling deep, fruitful discussions during breakout sessions. These sessions created a valuable opportunity to hear firsthand from customers – their perspectives on the presented functionality, as well as their concerns, expectations, and ideas around AI use cases and future innovations.

The council was opened with an executive overview of the Cloud ERP SCM strategy from Vatsan Govindarajan (CPO Cloud ERP SCM), setting the strategic direction and context for the innovations and discussions that followed.

AI was a central theme throughout the event. Sessions such as:

  • Insights into Combined Orders and AI-supported Backorder Processing led by Andreas Krause and Sarah Deser
  • Driving Efficiency in Inventory Management Through AI led by Darius Nowak, Stefanie Bierbach and Bernd Rödel
  • AI Support for Supplier Certificates and Complaint Processing  led by Stefan Resag

illustrated how SAP is moving beyond traditional automation toward intelligent, outcome-driven supply chain execution.
The sessions demonstrated how agents can replace manual, time-intensive, and reactive work across multiple applications through proactive assessments and intelligent recommendations – freeing up capacity to focus on higher-value, critical tasks.

Customer contributions, including “Acceleron – AI Use Case Test Automation”,  further reinforced the importance of co-innovation and real-world validation.

At the same time, core business capabilities remained in focus where colleagues presented updates in AVC such as Solution Bundling app, innovations for Asset Management​ and the direction for Advanced Production Planning ​in cloud.

Breakout sessions enabled open dialogue and deeper collaboration, ensuring that customer feedback directly shapes SAP’s product direction. In my interactive AI Talk, we gathered valuable insights into customer entry barriers for AI adoption such as cost and licensing model, agent accountability, received immediate feedback on the demonstrated AI use cases, and gained a deeper understanding of customers’ vision and expectations for AI capabilities across the supply chain.

Thanks to all participants for their openness, engagement, and valuable contributions. 

And a big thank you to the core organizational team Markus Oertelt and Fernanda Rodrigues for the smooth organization, a well-curated agenda, a picturesque venue, and outstanding ongoing support that made this event a truly seamless and inspiring experience!

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Dortmund, Germany)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for  Friday June 12th, Dortmund Germany  from 10:00 - 16:00 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Location:  Uniorg,  Lissaboner Allee 8, 44269 Dortmund, Germany

The instructor is @Andre_Fischer 

Here are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

Getting there & parking

We have reserved 10 parking spaces on-site for attendees. Please be aware that due to ongoing construction works, overall parking availability on our premises is currently limited.

As an alternative, there is a multi-storey car park directly opposite the Edeka supermarket near our office. Please note that this car park accepts cash only — no card payment is available, so we recommend coming prepared accordingly.

We look forward to a great day of learning and coding with you!

Bonus tip: The day after the CodeJam, on Saturday, June 13, 2026, the SAP Inside Track NRW 2026 takes place — also in Dortmund. 

If you're already in town, this is a great opportunity to extend your trip! In the evening, there will be a pre-event : SAP Stammtisch Dortmund & Cologne/Düsseldorf at Hövels Brauerei starting at 6:00 PM. You can find all the details at the bottom of the event page: https://sapstammtisch.github.io/Dortmund/sitNRW-2026

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Become an SAP Certified Security Administrator!
entry.content:
The new hands-on practical examination for becoming an SAP Certified Security Administrator was released earlier this month and has been well received! This certification verifies that the candidate has a general understanding of the core knowledge required of a Security Administrator in SAP system security. It proves that the candidate has a basic understanding about SAP authorization and security concepts in SAP S/4HANA Public and Private Edition and can put this knowledge into practice as a member of a project or security team.

We are pleased to announce today that we will be offering “Get Certified” Live Sessions to help prepare candidates for this examination starting May 27, 2026.  In these live sessions, expert trainers will support you via a number of live sessions related to the C_SEC certification: SAP Certified - Security Administrator.

The series consists of the following:

  • Kick-off session - Outlining the live session series, introducing the certification, explaining the certification format and exam interface, providing tips for preparation, explaining the learning journey self-study content, practice systems, and corresponding content review sessions - 1 hour
  • 8 content review sessions - Covering important certification-relevant topics from the courses that are part of the corresponding learning journey.  These sessions complement your self-study, providing you with the opportunity to get explanations and demonstrations from an expert, who can answer questions you have about the content, helping you to be better prepared for the certification.

 

    • How to set up and maintain users in SAP S/4HANA
    • Working with ABAP Role Maintenance
    • Understanding Authorization Default Values in SU24
    • Managing SAP Fiori Catalogs
    • Managing SAP Fiori Business Roles
    • SAP Fiori Spaces and Pages
    • SAP Authorizations - Traces
    • SAP Authorizations - SAP User Information System Reports

 

Sessions can be taken in any order, as your busy schedule permits.

Sessions are offered in multiple time zones, so if you are interested in becoming an SAP Certified Security Administrator, check out the link below to sign up for the sessions you’re interested in:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-sap-certified-security-administrator?searchId=51f47223-b967-47f6-85d9-f14d46cce51e&listPosition=1

The complete SAP Learning Journey for this certification is found at:

Managing User Access and Security of SAP S/4 Hana and SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Federating queries to Databricks from SAP Datasphere for real-time analytics in SAP Analytics Cl
entry.content:
@Sangeetha_K1  

Thank you for the detailed blog. Our requirement is actually the other way around. We want to develop reports and perform ML in Databricks using non-SAP data, while also consuming SAP data from SAP Datasphere.

We have tested this integration using BDC Connect / Delta Sharing, and it works well. We would like to understand whether the SDI Data Provisioning (DP) Agent can also be used to expose SAP Datasphere data to Databricks as a remote table. Based on this, we want to compare the Delta Sharing approach with the Data Provisioning Agent–based sharing approach. 

We wanted to check whether you have already implemented or tested this setup, and any guidance or best practices you can share would be greatly appreciated

entry.title: Katowice - SAP Stammtisch 24.06.2026
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community Katowice Members,

We are pleased to invite you to the summer SAP Stammtisch in Katowice, an informal networking meeting dedicated to exchanging experiences, discussing current SAP topics, and strengthening connections within our local community.

The SAP Stammtisch is a relaxed, open event and a great opportunity to meet fellow SAP professionals, share insights from ongoing projects, and stay connected with what’s happening in the SAP ecosystem.

Date: Wednesday, 23 June 2026

Time: 18:00

Venue: Woda Beach Bar, Katowice
(tour summer meeting place)

In addition to networking, we would like to use this meeting to open a discussion with the Community about ideas for future SAP-related initiatives in our region. We would like to talk about how we can encourage each other to closer collaboration, increase engagement, and gain stronger support from our employers. In short, our goal is to make SAP Community Katowice more active and visible, and we believe this can only be achieved through interesting initiatives and the involvement of all community members.

Whether you are an experienced SAP expert or just starting your journey, you are very welcome to join us. There is no formal agenda — just open discussion, shared ideas, and a friendly atmosphere.

Please confirm your participation by selecting “I will attend”, which will help us plan the event and encourage others to join.

Kind regards, and see you soon!

If you are not a member of the Katowice Group, please feel invited to join us: SAP Community Katowice

entry.title: Re: Pm Order TECO Resrtiction
entry.content: Yes you can use User Exit - IWO10004 go to SMOD tcode and enter the "IWO10004" in the components you will see the Function Module "EXIT_SAPLCOIH_004" in this you can add ABAP logic to check if Permit is open or closed (Using work order AUFK-AUFNR get AUFK-OBJNR pass to IHGNS-OBJNR and the IHGNS-GENIAKT field is X, the permit is considered closed otherwise it is Issued and Active)

entry.title: Re: EXCEPTION SEGMENT UNKNOWN RAISED error while calling RFC IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE in Biztalk Middl
entry.content:
The fastest way to make progress here might be to just open IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE in SE37, set an "external breakpoint" for the ABAP user used in BizTalk, and then start the call from BizTalk.

The exception SEGMENT_UNKNOWN could indeed mean what it says (e.g. BizTalk misspelling the segment name or using a wrong input), but I have also seen IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE throwing this error, if the ABAP user had insufficient permissions.... 🙂  (But in that case I would assume, that every segment lookup would fail, not only those for IDocs of release < 7.58? Unless BizTalk uses a different user for these two kinds of IDocs?!)

So if there is only one ABAP user defined in BizTalk, then most probably BizTalk is doing something wrong, like using the E2 name instead of the E1 name, etc. That should then be visible in the ABAP debugger, when inspecting the inputs that BizTalk feeds into IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE.

(The bug that IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE throws SEGMENT_UNKNOWN when in fact the authorizations are missing, has been fixed a long time ago, and as you say, the backend is S/4, it most probably already has that fix. So I think the chances are quite high that BizTalk is doing something wrong here.)

But honestly: this BizTalk version looks very very old! It is still using the classic RFC library, which has been out-of-maintenance since 2016. (Support for Classic RFC Library Ends March 2016 SAP Blogs) I think Microsoft switched from classic RFC Lib to .NET Connector more than 10 years ago. Get the latest version, instead of wasting time with possible bugs that may have been fixed 10 years ago...!

PS: according to Microsoft, the BizTalk SAP Adapter was updated from classic RFC Lib to NCo with release "BizTalk 2013R2 Cumulative Update 2", which came 2015. So you are running a BizTalk version older than 2015. Considering how many bugs have been fixed in BizTalk since then, you may really be wasting your time here using these out-dated components.

entry.title: Re: I am getting a warning message while opening a work o...
entry.content:
What you describing is SAP Assembly/Material Deletion Flag Behavior:

Header Level: Deletion flags on equipment, functional location, or assembly/material trigger immediate validation warnings in IW31/IW32 (Create/Change Maintenance Order). These are warning messages, not blocking, but can be configured using transaction OMCQ.

Component Level: For materials or assemblies added to the components tab of work orders, deletion flag validation is deferred until downstream transactions like MIGO (goods movement) or MRP runs execute. 

Navigation: IW32 > Header Data tab > Reference Object > Assembly

Assembly Defaulting: Work order assemblies automatically default through the Construction Type field ("ConstType") on the Functional Location or Equipment, which references either a direct material number, an assembly with Material BOM, or an Equipment BOM assembly. This construction type configuration links the equipment/location to the appropriate assembly and BOM structure that populates during work order creation.

Hope this is Helpful !

entry.title: Re: EXCEPTION SEGMENT UNKNOWN RAISED error while calling RFC IDOCTYPE_READ_COMPLETE in Biztalk Middl
entry.content:
PS: another idea: as MS BizTalk will reach end-of-life in 2028 anyway, perhaps you can even use a different EDI tool for your IDoc processing needs?

I can recommend SAP Business Connector:

  • It's included for free in any standard SAP license
  • It has very similar functionality as BizTalk (e.g. in this case: receive IDocs from S/4, convert them into some other data format and then forward to some external receiver via HTTP, FTP, Email, JDBC, etc.)
  • You get support for it by simply opening an SAP OSS ticket under component BC-MID-BUS

If you are still at the beginning of your project and not bound to BizTalk for some reason, you could give it a try.
Download is available at
​​https://support.sap.com/sbc-download
​Documentation:
​SAP Business Connector in Detail
